sceneTest.primaryQuestion: Can the viewer see a real core plus attached support systems producing agent behavior?

chapters.silhouettePlan: Make the model core a large rounded engine silhouette; keep memory as a tall rack, tool ports as a low connector bank, safety relays as a narrow warning cabinet, and the user-facing behavior as a directional output chute so roles are recognizable by outline alone.
chapters.propDensity: Add 4 supporting props: gauge cluster, relay lines, plug-port circles, and a stability/capacity readout near the core; keep them quieter than the hot path.
chapters.asymmetryPlan: Keep the core near center but cluster memory/tool systems to the left and safety/console systems to the right so the room feels inhabited rather than perfectly radial.
chapters.antiCardFraming: Do not frame every subsystem as identical cards; use one rack, one port bank, one relay cabinet, and open connector rails around the core.
chapters.sceneTexture: Use subtle machine-room floor wiring, short connector rails, and gauge marks to make the environment feel infrastructural without turning into background clutter.

premiumSceneTest.primaryQuestion: Does the scene feel like a real machine room around a model core rather than a hub-and-spoke box diagram?

chapters.silhouettePlan: Make the director booth elevated and narrow, the cue rail long and horizontal, the prop deck wide and shelf-like, the script card compact and paper-like, and the actor/stage area visibly theatrical.
chapters.propDensity: Add 4 supporting props: hanging cues, stacked props, stage rails, and curtain edges; keep props lighter than the director-to-output hot path.
chapters.asymmetryPlan: Bias the stage action slightly rightward so the output curtain feels like the performance destination while the script and props sit lower and left of the actor.
chapters.antiCardFraming: Avoid turning prompt/context/harness into three stacked info cards; preserve an actual stage world with open space, rails, booth, and props.
chapters.sceneTexture: Use subtle curtain edges, stage borders, cue drops, and deck lines so the scene reads like a theater stack instead of a layered UI.

premiumSceneTest.primaryQuestion: Does the learner perceive a theater world with stacked control responsibilities rather than three framed layers?

sceneTest.primaryQuestion: Can the viewer instantly see what gets through, what is discarded, and what capacity is constrained?

chapters.silhouettePlan: Make the intake a pile of crates, the screening gate a barred checkpoint, the accepted path a truck silhouette with visible capacity, the reject path a loose dump pile, and the side path a small courier form.
chapters.propDensity: Add 5 supporting props: crate variation, gate bars, truck wheels, capacity stripe, and courier wheels; keep them subordinate to the intake-gate-truck hot path.
chapters.asymmetryPlan: Keep the accepted path strong and horizontal, push the reject pile downward, and place the courier side lane off-axis so the composition immediately shows priority vs side handling.
chapters.antiCardFraming: Do not collapse input/filter/output into three boxes; preserve open cargo flow, loose piles, and a vehicle silhouette.
chapters.sceneTexture: Use subtle floor-lane marks, gate bars, and truck body details to make the screening metaphor feel physical rather than abstract.

premiumSceneTest.primaryQuestion: Is it obvious at a glance what gets accepted, what gets discarded, and what capacity constrains the working set?

chapters.silhouettePlan: Make the work item rounded and movable, the build bench table-like, the test gate diamond-like and central, the visual station inspection-like, the judge distinct, and the rework chute clearly chute-shaped.
chapters.propDensity: Add 4 supporting props: bench markings, inspection lens/visual marks, pass tray lip, and rework lane rails; keep them secondary to the QA lane.
chapters.asymmetryPlan: Keep the main QA lane straight and dominant, place pass higher/right, and bend rework downward and back so the failure route feels physically more costly.
chapters.antiCardFraming: Do not turn verification into a row of framed labels; preserve a work lane with distinct stations and a spatially meaningful rework branch.
chapters.sceneTexture: Use subtle lane markings, bench edges, inspection detail, and chute rails to give the QA path a factory-floor feel.

premiumSceneTest.primaryQuestion: Can a learner see that nothing is complete until it passes through multiple inspection stages and that failure loops back?

chapters.silhouettePlan: Make the manager desk broad and supervisory, the dossier tray compact and handoff-like, specialist rooms door-based and isolated, the summary mailbox return-focused, and the blocked corridor visibly restrictive.
chapters.propDensity: Add 4 supporting props: room doors, control clipboard, dossier tray, and mailbox slot; keep them quieter than the delegation/return flow.
chapters.asymmetryPlan: Let delegation split upward and downward into separate rooms while the summary return converges rightward; keep the blocked corridor low and off the main path.
chapters.antiCardFraming: Avoid making subagents a tree of rectangles; preserve real rooms, doorways, and return artifacts that communicate controlled delegation.
chapters.sceneTexture: Use subtle office walls, door edges, corridor spacing, and desk surfaces so the scene reads as a project office rather than an org chart.

premiumSceneTest.primaryQuestion: Does the scene communicate bounded delegation with controlled summary return, not just branching decomposition?

sceneTest.primaryQuestion: Can the viewer see these as control decisions with consequences rather than a static list of principles?

chapters.silhouettePlan: Make the console wide and control-centric, levers slender and varied, gauges circular, the selected output lane directional, and the locked mode rail restrictive and warning-colored.
chapters.propDensity: Add 4 supporting props: indicator lights, gauge needles, console panel lines, and lock/warning marks near the restricted mode rail.
chapters.asymmetryPlan: Keep the lever bank clustered left/center, spread gauges rightward, and place the locked mode rail below the hot path so consequence and restriction read differently.
chapters.antiCardFraming: Do not render the seven decisions as a checklist of cards; preserve the idea of a live control console where choices alter machine behavior.
chapters.sceneTexture: Use subtle panel seams, indicator lights, and gauge markings so the console feels operative without overwhelming the levers.

premiumSceneTest.primaryQuestion: Does the scene feel like a control room where architectural decisions tune behavior, rather than a static list of principles?
